bugs.error_message: E2E getJwt() sends {user_id} body and reads data.access_token — both wrong for Java iam-service API
bugs.root_cause: Python user-mgmt POST /auth/token expected {user_id: string} and returned {access_token}. Java iam-service expects {username, password} and returns {accessToken}.
bugs.fix: Changed body to {username: userId, password: 'Meridian@2024'} and read data.accessToken

bugs.error_message: 03-jwt-identity.spec.ts: health, JWKS, token, membership tests all fail after iam-service migration
bugs.root_cause: Tests were written for the Python user-mgmt service. Java iam-service uses GET /actuator/health, /oauth2/jwks (no 'use'/'alg'/'kid' fields), {username,password} token request, and PATCH /users/{id}/book for membership changes.
bugs.fix: Rewrote all 4 test categories for the Java iam-service API shape

bugs.error_message: 08-domain-authz.spec.ts: rm_jane and rm_diaz JWT fetch uses {user_id} and body.access_token
bugs.root_cause: Direct /auth/token calls at lines 231-265 still used old Python API contract
bugs.fix: Changed to {username, password} body and body.accessToken field

bugs.error_message: AuthController JWT missing segments, clearance, book, admin_domains, audience claims
bugs.root_cause: JWT issued by iam-service lacked claims needed by Cerbos policy evaluation and gateway identity resolution
bugs.fix: Added ObjectMapper injection, extractClearance/extractSegments helpers, book/adminDomains from UserResponse, audience(['meridian-gateway']) to JWT builder

bugs.error_message: PATCH /users/{id}/book returns 500: column 'metadata' is of type jsonb but expression is of type character varying
bugs.root_cause: Hibernate maps String field to character varying by default. The column is jsonb. Inserting null causes a type mismatch.

bugs.error_message: Gateway returns 401 for valid iam-service JWT: invalid iss and missing aud claim
bugs.root_cause: Gateway VALID_ISSUERS was hardcoded to ['meridian-user-mgmt', 'http://user-mgmt:8084']. iam-service JWT has iss=http://iam-service:8084 and no aud claim.
bugs.fix: Made required-issuers configurable via @Value (includes all iam-service URL variants). Added audience(['meridian-gateway']) to AuthController JWT builder.

bugs.error_message: JwksClient fails with 'Invalid JSON object': Java HttpClient does not follow redirects by default
bugs.root_cause: Java HttpClient.Redirect.NEVER is the default. /.well-known/jwks.json returns 301 to /oauth2/jwks. HttpClient got the redirect response (HTML/empty body), JWKSet.parse() failed.
bugs.fix: Added .followRedirects(HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL) to HttpClient builder. Also changed MERIDIAN_AUTH_JWKS_URL in docker-compose to point directly to /oauth2/jwks.

bugs.error_message: Cerbos denies rm_jane all wealth agents: clearance-pii threshold was 3, rm_jane has clearance 2
bugs.root_cause: Cerbos policy required clearance >= 3 for confidential-pii. All wealth agents are data_classification=confidential-pii. rm_jane (and all standard RMs) have clearance 2.
bugs.fix: Changed confidential-pii threshold from >= 3 to >= 2. Portfolio PII is a core RM function; restricted trading data (clearance 3) is the right cutoff for 'restricted'.

bugs.error_message: E2E users rm_diaz and da_wpb do not exist in iam-service database
bugs.root_cause: Tests reference rm_diaz (dual-segment RM) and da_wpb (domain_admin) but only rm_jane was seeded in V2__seed_demo_data.sql
bugs.fix: Created new Flyway migration V3__seed_e2e_users.sql seeding both users with correct roles and attributes (clearance, segments, admin_domains)

bugs.error_message: 04-entitlements.spec.ts:12 and :51 timeout — 'Target page, context or browser has been closed' at helpers.ts:109
bugs.root_cause: Playwright test timeout was 90_000ms. LibreChat browser UI tests require ~15-20s login + ~60-80s LLM synthesis = 75-100s, exceeding the timeout. When timeout fires, Playwright closes the page context and page.waitForTimeout() throws 'page closed'.
bugs.fix: Increased timeout from 90_000 to 180_000ms in playwright.config.ts. Both UI tests now complete in ~90-96s and pass within 180s.

bugs.error_message: principal.book() accessor used in ChatService.handleClarify, test files (AuthzFromMembershipTest, RoleAuthorizationTest, SecurityRejectionIT) after book removed from Principal record
bugs.root_cause: book field removed from Principal record (architectural fix) but callsites were not updated simultaneously

bugs.error_message: DomainManifestStore init fails: named capturing group is missing trailing } — Failed to parse domain manifest wealth-management.json
bugs.root_cause: resolveEnvVars() called Matcher.appendReplacement() with unquoted m.group(0) as fallback when env var was not set. m.group(0) contains ${WEALTH_COVERAGE_URL} which Matcher.appendReplacement interprets as a named back-reference, throwing IllegalArgumentException.
bugs.fix: Changed fallback to use Matcher.quoteReplacement(m.group(0)) so ${ is treated as literal text.

bugs.error_message: Insurance query naming POL-77001 wrongly clarifies 'which policy?' once a second domain is onboarded; gateway checks relationship_id instead of policy_id
bugs.root_cause: handleFetchData derived coverageEntity from coverageEntityType() which uses manifestStore.requiredContextKeys()/entityTypes() — the cross-domain UNION across ALL sub-domains — and picked the first key (relationship_id from wealth). The coverage entity was never scoped to the routed sub-domain.
bugs.fix: Added DomainManifestStore.entityTypesFor(subDomainId) (single sub-domain's entity_types) and scoped overloads coverageEntityType(EffectiveManifest)/secondaryEntityType(EffectiveManifest). In handleFetchData, once the resource-scoped agent's EffectiveManifest em is known, re-scope coverageEntity/secondaryEntity/carriedCoverageId from em.requiredContext()+em.subDomainId() before the coverage resolve/check/bind. Follow-up/session paths now use carriedCoverageEntityType(session) which finds whichever required entity the session actually carries (domain-agnostic).

bugs.error_message: LibreChat OIDC SSO: 'Callback authentication failed — server responded with an error in the response body'; login failed for rm_jane
bugs.root_cause: TWO bugs. (1) Client meridian-librechat registered only for CLIENT_SECRET_BASIC, but LibreChat's openid-client sends credentials via client_secret_post -> Spring AS rejected with 'Client authentication failed: authentication_method' -> invalid_client. (2) id_token/userinfo carried only `sub` because the JWT customizer enriched ACCESS_TOKEN only; LibreChat needs email/name to provision the user.
bugs.fix: (1) Added ClientAuthenticationMethod.CLIENT_SECRET_POST to the librechat RegisteredClient (alongside BASIC). (2) Extended JwtClaimsCustomizer to handle ID_TOKEN and added OidcClaimEnricher.enrichIdToken() returning email, email_verified, preferred_username, name (from principal + display_name attr). Verified full flow via curl: client_secret_post exchange -> HTTP 200, id_token+userinfo carry email/name, LibreChat callback -> 'login success', openid user created in Mongo.
